include_rationale: Near two, feelings arrive faster than words. Colour sorting gives a shared label without a long talk. Borrow first if friends already own it, then buy if it becomes the nightly ask. Keep it nearby so they can choose it themselves.

Near two, moods swing hard. Todd Parr faces are easy to point at after a wobble when words lag. Distinct from Colour Monster because faces do the teaching, not colour jars. Keep it nearby so they can choose it themselves.

product_name: In My Heart: A Book of Feelings
include_rationale: Near two, big feelings need a gentle frame. Heart shaped pages make the talk tactile: shared looking, not a long explanation. Strong when Colour Monster is already on the shelf. Keep it nearby so they can choose it themselves. Share it together for a few minutes, then let them lead.

Near two, naming the good days helps too. One feeling per book keeps the page short after a park win. First Child's Play title; pair with the sad title only if you want a set. Keep it nearby so they can choose it themselves.

rank_rationale: #5: sad naming board; second Child's Play.

Near two, grumpy arrives as a full body weather system. A funny frog story names it without a scolding tone. Good when board feelings sets feel thin and you want a proper little story. Keep it nearby so they can choose it themselves.

Near two, mine fights arrive daily. This story shows the squabble and the mend without a heavy moral. Useful when face books are covered and you need a turn taking tale. Keep it nearby so they can choose it themselves.
